text.title: field-descriptions-as-prompts Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for field descriptions as micro-prompts.

rule.statement: Every non-trivial field (anything beyond a self-explanatory enum) MUST have a `description` value of 1-3 sentences.
rule.rationale: Descriptions cost ~10 tokens each and pay for themselves; PARSE measured 60%+ extraction accuracy lift from optimised descriptions.

rule.statement: Each description MUST cover, in order: (a) what the field represents, (b) format / units / range when non-obvious, (c) edge cases (missing/ambiguous input), (d) at least one forbidden pattern observed in past mis-fillings.
rule.rationale: All four elements collectively close the failure-mode space; missing one leaks a class of errors.

rule.statement: When a field depends on a prior field (summary derived from body, slug derived from title), the description MUST name the dependent field by its backtick-wrapped name.
rule.rationale: Re-grounding the model on the named dependency makes the dependency executable instead of implied.

rule.statement: Descriptions MUST NOT instruct the model to explain its reasoning; reasoning belongs in a separate scratchpad field declared before the answer.
rule.rationale: Asking for reasoning inside the value field corrupts the answer shape; split into reasoning + answer fields.

rule.statement: Descriptions MUST NOT contradict the field name or type; if they would, rename the field instead.
rule.rationale: Contradiction (e.g. `amount: float` described as "string of cents") confuses the model and the consumer; fix the name, not the description.
